chest.name: Push Ups
chest.tips: Keep your body in a straight line. Don't let hips sag.

chest.name: Incline Push Ups
chest.tips: Use a bench or elevated surface to reduce difficulty.

chest.name: Dumbbell Bench Press
chest.tips: Lower weights until elbows are at 90°. Control the descent.

back.name: Assisted Pull Ups
back.tips: Use an assisted pull-up machine or resistance band for help.

back.name: Dumbbell Rows
back.tips: Keep your back flat. Pull elbow back towards hip.

back.name: Lat Pulldown
back.tips: Keep chest up and pull the bar to your upper chest.

legs.name: Bodyweight Squats
legs.tips: Keep chest up, weight on heels. Knees track over toes.

legs.name: Lunges
legs.tips: Step forward, lower back knee toward floor. Stay upright.

legs.name: Leg Press
legs.tips: Don't lock knees at the top. Keep feet shoulder-width apart.

shoulders.name: Dumbbell Shoulder Press
shoulders.tips: Press straight up, don't arch back excessively.

shoulders.name: Lateral Raises
shoulders.tips: Use light weight. Raise to shoulder height only.

arms.name: Dumbbell Bicep Curls
arms.tips: Keep elbows close to sides. Don't swing the weight.

arms.name: Tricep Pushdowns
arms.tips: Keep elbows fixed. Fully extend arms at the bottom.

chest.name: Barbell Bench Press
chest.tips: Arch back slightly, retract shoulder blades. Touch bar to lower chest.

chest.name: Incline Dumbbell Press
chest.tips: Set bench at 30–45°. Lower to upper chest.

chest.name: Cable Flyes
chest.tips: Maintain slight elbow bend throughout. Feel the stretch.

back.name: Pull Ups
back.tips: Full range of motion. Chin over bar, arms fully extended at bottom.

back.name: Barbell Rows
back.tips: Hinge at hips, back parallel to floor. Pull bar to belly button.

back.name: Seated Cable Row
back.tips: Keep chest up. Pull to lower abdomen, squeeze at peak.

legs.name: Barbell Squats
legs.tips: Bar on upper traps. Break parallel, drive through heels.

legs.name: Romanian Deadlift
legs.tips: Keep bar close to legs. Hinge at hips, slight knee bend.

legs.name: Bulgarian Split Squat
legs.tips: Rear foot elevated on bench. Keep front shin vertical.

chest.name: Barbell Bench Press (Heavy)
chest.tips: Use spotter for heavy sets. Explosive concentric, controlled eccentric.

chest.name: Weighted Dips
chest.tips: Lean forward for chest emphasis. Full range of motion.

back.name: Weighted Pull Ups
back.tips: Use weight belt or hold dumbbell between feet.

back.name: Deadlift
back.tips: Neutral spine throughout. Hip hinge pattern. Bar stays over mid-foot.

legs.name: Front Squat
legs.tips: Elbows high, bar on front delts. More upright torso than back squat.

beforeWorkout.title: Dynamic Warm-Up
beforeWorkout.tip: Spend 5–10 minutes doing light cardio and dynamic stretches to increase heart rate and blood flow to muscles.

beforeWorkout.title: Hydration
beforeWorkout.tip: Drink 16–20 oz of water 2 hours before workout. Sip 8 oz every 15–20 minutes during exercise.

beforeWorkout.title: Mobility Work
beforeWorkout.tip: Perform joint circles and mobility drills specific to the muscles you'll be training for better range of motion.

duringWorkout.title: Proper Form
duringWorkout.tip: Always prioritize form over weight. Poor technique leads to injury and reduces training effectiveness.

duringWorkout.title: Breathing
duringWorkout.tip: Exhale during the effort (concentric), inhale during the release (eccentric). Never hold your breath.

duringWorkout.title: Progressive Overload
duringWorkout.tip: Gradually increase weight, reps, or sets each week to continuously challenge your muscles and drive adaptation.

afterWorkout.title: Cool-Down & Stretching
afterWorkout.tip: Spend 5–10 minutes doing static stretches for trained muscles. Hold each stretch for 20–30 seconds.

afterWorkout.title: Recovery
afterWorkout.tip: Allow 48 hours rest for each muscle group. Sleep 7–9 hours for optimal recovery and muscle growth.

afterWorkout.title: Protein Intake
afterWorkout.tip: Consume 20–40g of protein within 30–60 minutes post-workout to support muscle repair and growth.

macros.carbs: Adjust based on activity level and goals

tips: Eat whole, unprocessed foods 80% of the time
Don't skip breakfast — fuel your morning training
Meal prep on weekends to stay consistent
Track your calories for at least 4 weeks to build awareness
Fiber is your friend — aim for 25–35g daily
Eat 5–6 small meals or 3 balanced meals — whatever works for you
